name: Rejuvelac
subtitle: The "Life Water" from sprouted grains
blrNote: Use Wheat berries or Quinoa. Don’t use "polished" grains—they won’t sprout. Any local health food store in Indiranagar will have raw wheat.
tldr: Sprout grains, soak them for 2 days. Drink the cloudy, lemony liquid. Great for vegan cheese.

steps.title: Sprout
steps.instruction: Soak grains overnight. Drain and leave in a jar until tiny "tails" (sprouts) appear.

steps.title: Soak
steps.instruction: Put sprouted grains in a jar with 1L water. Cover with cloth.

steps.title: Ferment
steps.instruction: Wait 24–48 hours until the water is cloudy and smells slightly like lemon and yeast.

images.caption: Rejuvelac
images.alt: Rejuvelac — The "Life Water" from sprouted grains

culturalContext.story: Popularized by Ann Wigmore, the founder of the raw food movement.
culturalContext.significance: It is a potent "starter" for vegan nut cheeses. If you want to make cashew brie, you need this liquid.
culturalContext.funFact: It’s essentially "pre-beer." If you added more sugar and hops, you’d be brewing.
